scenario_results.name: Site-to-site VPN intake and topology qualification
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves intake acceptance only after topology, address space, traffic selector, route-domain, failover, source, and approval evidence is validated.

scenario_results.name: Missing prerequisite information before intake can be accepted
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves blocked intake and missing-record handling when tenant, platform, authorization, routing, DNS/IPAM, and logging facts are absent.

scenario_results.name: Invalid or ineligible remote-access request
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of access approval authority and routing to accountable identity or access owners before network guidance is treated as actionable.

scenario_results.name: Remote-user split tunnel, DNS, and address-pool guidance
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves source-backed split tunnel, DNS, address-pool, route exception, monitoring, assumption, risk, and rollback guidance.

scenario_results.name: Gateway/client integration and route-policy edge case
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves configuration review output for gateway/client integration, route symmetry, authorization rules, DNS behavior, and tenant source-of-truth conflicts.

scenario_results.name: Tunnel flapping and rekey fault isolation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves incident triage for IKEv2 negotiation, NAT traversal, DPD/keepalive, clocks, cipher mismatch, route, and path stability causes.

scenario_results.name: MTU and fragmentation failure
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves edge-case connectivity handling for encapsulation overhead, PMTUD, MSS, ESP/TLS considerations, measurement, and rollback.

scenario_results.name: Protocol and deployment-mode comparison
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves protocol and deployment comparison across IKEv2/IPsec, TLS VPN, OpenVPN-compatible service, WireGuard, and cloud-native client VPN.

scenario_results.name: Gateway or region failover and availability review
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves availability guidance for failover sequence, route and DNS dependencies, health checks, user impact, blast radius, and authority escalation.

scenario_results.name: Certificate and key lifecycle incident
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves lifecycle incident runbook output for certificate/key rotation, overlap windows, revocation, rollback, and approval boundaries.

scenario_results.name: Monitoring and observability expectations
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves monitoring blueprint for tunnel state, handshake latency, authentication events, DNS, packet loss, owner response, and retention caveats.

scenario_results.name: Boundary rejection for application-only troubleshooting
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves clear rejection of application code and frontend/backend troubleshooting unrelated to network transport.

scenario_results.name: Boundary rejection for endpoint or MDM workflow
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of endpoint management, MDM, and EDR policy ownership while preserving tunnel-transport handoff facts.

scenario_results.name: Boundary rejection for cloud identity policy ownership
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves routing of conditional access, MFA, group entitlement, and identity lifecycle design away from the VPN networking lane.

scenario_results.name: Boundary rejection for firewall signature tuning and SOC investigation
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves refusal of firewall signature tuning, DLP policy, SOC investigation, incident closure, and routing to specialized adjacent lanes.

scenario_results.name: Low-confidence escalation for unsupported vendor preview behavior
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves low-confidence escalation, source freshness handling, vendor evidence request, and lab validation/human review path.

scenario_results.name: Conflicting source or tenant policy handling
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves source-policy conflict handling and orchestrator return when public provider guidance conflicts with tenant routing policy.

scenario_results.name: Tenant-specific adaptation without generic drift
scenario_results.evidence: Scenario proves cross-industry materiality, tenant-specific adaptation, data classification, approval matrix, rollback, specification-based runtime, and promotion criteria.

notes: All 18 refreshed scenarios passed against the validated standard validation profile.

Coverage includes design, configuration review, fault isolation, performance and availability tradeoffs, edge-case failures, prerequisite gaps, ineligible requests, adjacent routing, low-confidence escalation, conflicting-source escalation, tenant adaptation, and no unreviewed regressions.
The prior built package is superseded as baseline context by this spec-first package.
